items.value: reads credential files such as .npmrc, .git-credentials, cloud credentials, kube config, SSH keys, browser profiles, wallet files
items.description: package code should rarely inspect user credential stores

items.value: enumerates or exfiltrates environment variables containing API keys, tokens, cloud credentials, or LLM provider keys
contacts cloud metadata services such as 169.254.169.254
sends files, env vars, tokens, system info, or scan results to an external HTTP endpoint, webhook, paste site, raw IP, or newly created repo
downloads remote code or binaries and then evals, imports, writes, chmods, or executes them
executes dynamically decoded strings using eval, Function, vm, exec, compile, import-from-string, or equivalent
hides behavior using base64/hex/charcode arrays, XOR loops, packed/minified payloads, or opaque string construction
spawns shell commands or tools such as curl, wget, bash, sh, powershell, nc, python, node, chmod, or system package managers
uses install-time or startup execution such as npm preinstall/install/postinstall, setup.py side effects, .pth import lines, or Python import-time side effects
items.description: code runs without normal app invocation

items.value: creates cron jobs, startup services, shell profile hooks, GitHub Actions workflows, self-hosted runners, or registry/system launch entries
uses package-manager tokens to enumerate maintainer packages and publish or upload modified packages
deletes home directories, wipes files/devices, encrypts data, or triggers destructive fallback behavior
